content:
Call Anthropic’s Claude models through the OpenAI-compatible Chat Completions format on Ace Data Cloud, with streaming, thinking models, vision, and working code in Python, JavaScript, and Java.

5 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

In our Claude API overview we introduced the two ways to call Claude on Ace Data Cloud: the native Messages API and the OpenAI-compatible Chat Completion format. Today we go deep on the second one, because it solves a very specific problem beautifully: you already have an app built on the OpenAI API, and you want Claude in it without rewriting anything.
That is the entire promise of the Claude Chat Completions API. Same request shape you already know, same response shape your code already parses, Claude underneath. Every example here is copy-paste ready.
Why this format exists
The OpenAI Chat Completions format became the industry standard, and thousands of apps, tutorials, and SDKs are built against it. Ace Data Cloud serves Claude through that exact format, so switching an existing app to Claude means changing the model name and the base URL. Your messages array, your choices parsing, your streaming handler, all of it keeps working.
If you are starting a brand-new project and want Claude’s full native feature set (the dedicated system field, native tool definitions, the thinking parameter), the Claude Messages API is the richer choice. For everything that already speaks OpenAI, this is the one.
Your first call
The endpoint is the familiar one:
POST https://api.acedata.cloud/v1/chat/completionsThree required pieces: your authorization header, a model, and a messages array where each message has a role (user, assistant, or system) and content. Around 20 Claude model options are available on this endpoint.
python
import requestsurl = "https://api.acedata.cloud/v1/chat/completions"headers = {
"accept": "application/json",
"authorization": "Bearer YOUR_TOKEN",
"content-type": "application/json"
}payload = {
"model": "claude-opus-4-20250514",
"messages": [{"role": "user", "content": "Hello"}]
}response = requests.post(url, json=payload, headers=headers)
print(response.text)The response comes back in the shape your OpenAI code already expects: the reply text lives in choices[0].message.content, finish_reason tells you why generation stopped, and a usage block reports prompt, completion, and total tokens for cost tracking.
Useful optional fields: max_tokens to cap the reply length, temperature (0 to 2) for randomness, n for multiple candidate replies, and response_format to set the return format.
Multi-turn conversations
Context works exactly like OpenAI: pass the whole conversation history in messages, alternating user and assistant turns.
python
payload = {
"model": "claude-opus-4-20250514",
"messages": [
{"role": "user", "content": "Hello"},
{"role": "assistant", "content": "Hello! How can I help you today?"},
{"role": "user", "content": "What did I say just now?"}
]
}Claude answers “You said Hello” because the full exchange travels with the request. Your existing conversation-management code needs zero changes.
Streaming
Set stream to true and the reply arrives as a series of data: chunks, each carrying the next piece of text in choices[0].delta.content, ending with data: [DONE]. It is the same Server-Sent Events format OpenAI uses, so any streaming handler you already wrote keeps working.
javascript
const options = {
method: "post",
headers: {
accept: "application/json",
authorization: "Bearer YOUR_TOKEN",
"content-type": "application/json",
},
body: JSON.stringify({
model: "claude-opus-4-20250514",
messages: [{ role: "user", content: "Hello" }],
stream: true,
}),
};fetch("https://api.acedata.cloud/v1/chat/completions", options)
.then((response) => response.json())
.then((response) => console.log(response))
.catch((err) => console.error(err));The final chunk before [DONE] carries the token usage, so you still get cost data on streamed calls.
Thinking models: reasoning included in the response
Here is where this endpoint gets genuinely special. Pick a thinking variant, such as claude-sonnet-4-20250514-thinking, and Claude reasons through the problem before answering, returning both parts:

• content: the final answer

• reasoning_content: the step-by-step thinking that produced it
Ask it for the sine of 30 degrees and you get the answer (1/2) plus the worked reasoning about the 30–60–90 triangle behind it. For math, analysis, and hard logic, the thinking variants visibly improve accuracy, and you switch to them the same way you switch any model: change one string.
Vision: send images through the same format
The claude-sonnet-4-20250514 model accepts images using the OpenAI-style content array:
python
payload = {
"model": "claude-sonnet-4-20250514",
"messages": [
{
"role": "user",
"content": [
{"type": "text", "text": "What's in this image?"},
{
"type": "image_url",
"image_url": {"url": "https://cdn.acedata.cloud/ueugot.png"}
}
]
}
]
}Claude reads the image and describes it in detail. If your app already sends images to OpenAI vision models, this exact structure carries over unchanged.
Handling errors
Standard codes: 400 for malformed requests, 401 for a missing or invalid token, 429 for rate limiting (retry with backoff), and 500 for temporary server issues. Every error response includes a trace_id worth logging, since it lets support pinpoint your exact request.
Pricing
Claude calls draw from the same shared credit system as everything on Ace Data Cloud: one General Balance across all 200+ models, topped up once. Billing follows token usage, and the usage block on every response shows exact counts, so the cost of each call is always visible. Calling Claude through Ace is typically cheaper than official provider pricing, and holding $ACE unlocks automatic usage discounts on top. Check the live pricing page for current per-model rates.
Getting started
Create an Ace Data Cloud account, copy your API token from the console, and run the first example above. If you are migrating an existing OpenAI-based app, the whole switch is: point the base URL at https://api.acedata.cloud/v1, set your Ace token, and change the model string to a Claude model. New accounts get free starter credit, so you can test the migration before spending anything.
Final thoughts
The Claude Chat Completions API is the shortest path from an OpenAI codebase to Claude: same format, same parsing, same streaming, with thinking models and vision available behind one model-string change. Combined with the native Messages API from our overview, you have both doors to Claude on Ace Data Cloud, behind one key and one balance.
Next in the series: more of the catalog, from video generation to music. Follow along so you do not miss it.
Start building at platform.acedata.cloud
